edges.claim: The UAE sent 30 tonnes of direct bilateral food aid to Kenya in February 2026 in response to La Niña-driven drought and food insecurity.

The UAE and Kenya signed a CEPA on 14 January 2025 — the UAE's first with a mainland African country — with terms finalised in June 2026 and implementation reaffirmed by Kenya's MFA in February 2026; it is signed but not yet in force per the UAE CEPA programme tracker.

Kenya–UAE bilateral trade reached Ksh 445 billion in 2023, with the UAE as Kenya's 6th-largest export destination and 2nd-largest import source (16% of imports); UAE–Kenya non-oil trade was USD 3.1 billion in 2023, up 26.4% year-on-year.
ADQ established a finance and investment framework with Kenya of up to USD 500 million targeting food production, mining, technology and logistics.
The Kenya–UAE Bilateral Investment Treaty (2014) is in force, providing the treaty basis for bilateral investment flows.
The UAE and Kenya jointly co-chaired (with the EU and Qatar) the 24 September 2025 ministerial meeting coordinating international Sudan de-escalation efforts, operating a joint co-chair mechanism.

The UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative for Africa — led by ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD, and co-led by the UAE foreign-aid agency — targets digital infrastructure across Africa, a programme scope that encompasses Kenya as an African partner named in Gulf-linked development coverage.

GAP: no evidence in the material documents any Masdar, DP World or AD Ports project, concession, or energy/infrastructure deployment in Kenya; project-level filings or announcements would be required to substantiate this category.
GAP: no evidence in the material quantifies or describes a UAE–Kenya remittance corridor; central-bank or balance-of-payments remittance data would be needed.

ADEX is the export-financing arm of the Abu Dhabi Fund for Development and leads the UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative, announced in a G20 context, linking UAE development finance to African digital infrastructure.
The UAE Office of Development Affairs partnered with the Tony Elumelu Foundation and the Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation to empower an additional 1,000 young African entrepreneurs.
The UAE pledged $500 million to a Sudan Humanitarian Fund in February 2026, amid famine conditions in Sudan confirmed by the Global Report on Food Crises 2026.
The UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned the targeting of a WFP humanitarian convoy in Sudan and publicly supports WFP humanitarian operations there.

Gaza is the largest recipient of UAE aid (~50% of all international aid entering Gaza), with more than $3 billion since 2023, 90,000+ tons delivered via 8,700 trucks, 13 ships and 80 airdrops, and ongoing convoy operations under Operation Chivalrous Knight 3 passing the 300-convoy milestone.

The UAE and EU held a Brussels panel on humanitarian supply-chain resilience on 11 June 2026, co-hosted by DG ECHO with more than 80 senior representatives, reflecting a working UAE–European Commission humanitarian-logistics dialogue.

The UAE and Qatar both co-signed the Gaza maritime corridor ministerial joint statement and co-chair the Sudan de-escalation mechanism, even as a 2024 academic study analyses them as rival Gulf donors in Sudan.
AD Ports Group and Masdar formed a partnership in November 2025 for global offshore wind logistics.

The UAE contributed $1.5 million in May 2026 to an ADB fund promoting agricultural innovation and food security in Asia-Pacific, made via the International Affairs Office at the UAE Presidential Court.

The UAE–India CEPA (signed February 2022) drove bilateral trade to USD 100 billion in FY2024-25 with a USD 200 billion target by 2032, serving as the benchmark template for CEPA impact relevant to the Kenya agreement.

Sheikh Theyab bin Mohamed bin Zayed — chairman of the restructured International Humanitarian and Philanthropic Council under Federal Decree No. 94 of 2026 — attended the International Development Dialogue hosted by the Office of Development Affairs.
UN OCHA's Financial Tracking Service independently tracks the UAE as donor #100, recording $94,336,382 in funded and $133,015,246 in paid UAE humanitarian flows for 2026.
